title: Implement backend product detail variant contract
verify: Backend product detail payload exposes product identity, media, option dimensions, variants/SKU, option combinations, price data, and availability/sellability signals.
Backend distinguishes product not found or unpublished from valid product detail responses.
Single/default SKU products have deterministic selected/default variant behavior without requiring extra option selection.

verify.summary: Required gates pass, but a multi-variant product with exactly one sellable variant is incorrectly treated as a single/default-SKU product. The payload sets requires_selection false and auto-selects the sellable SKU, conflicting with FT-002 explicit selection semantics for products with multiple concrete variants. Task closure is not eligible until the implementation and integration coverage are corrected.

TASK-011 passes reverification after default-selection remediation. The PostgreSQL-backed product-detail suite proves the required payload, unavailable and default-SKU behavior, and explicit selection for a multi-variant product with only one sellable variant. Independent reproduction, fixture cleanup, catalog regression, typecheck, Memory Bank lint, full T2 protocol, and required packet/spec gates pass. Manual T2 closure criteria are satisfied; FT-002 feature completion remains pending its remaining tasks and feature-level red verification.

evidence_required: Integration test output for product detail payload, variant combinations, availability, not-found/unpublished, and single/default SKU behavior.
Short note identifying native Medusa route versus thin read-only facade decision.
constraints: Use Medusa/PostgreSQL-backed product and variant data or supported Medusa mechanisms.
Use stable public product identifiers or handles where possible.
Do not expose internal database identifiers or implementation-only fields when stable public identifiers are available.
invariants: Backend/PostgreSQL owns product and variant source of truth.
Product detail availability is a pre-check and must not create reservation/order state.
purpose: Expose backend product detail and variant data needed by the storefront without duplicating variant truth.
success_outcome: Backend product detail reads prove required option/variant/availability semantics against seeded data.
anti_goals: Do not implement durable cart persistence or cart merge.
Do not implement checkout, payment, order, or inventory reservation behavior.
Do not add a custom product configurator framework.

stop_conditions: Native Medusa APIs cannot satisfy required product detail semantics and no thin facade can be scoped safely.
Endpoint contract would conflict with API guidelines.
Verification cannot prove product detail variant behavior against seeded backend data.
